metaTitle: Column Types — Format Numbers, Currency, Dates and More | Tablesmit
metaDescription: Set column types to format every cell consistently — Text, Number, Currency, Percentage, or Date. Click the column header to change. No formulas needed.
heroHeadline: Every column formatted consistently — with one click.
heroSubtext: Set a column type and every cell in that column formats automatically. Numbers right-align. Currency adds the symbol. Dates normalise. No formulas, no manual formatting.

benefits.heading: Types enforce consistency
benefits.body: A Number column right-aligns every cell and applies consistent decimal formatting. A Currency column adds the currency symbol. You set the type once — every cell follows.

benefits.heading: Change type from the column header
benefits.body: Click the type label in any column header (it shows 'Text' by default) to open the type selector. The entire column updates instantly.

benefits.heading: Sorting respects column type
benefits.body: Sort a Number column and the sort is numeric — 10 comes after 9, not before 2. Sort a Text column and the sort is alphabetical. The type determines the sort logic.

benefits.heading: Types carry through to Excel
benefits.body: Column type information is written to the Excel export. Number columns export as number cells. Date columns export as date cells — not text strings that look like dates.

columnTypes.name: Text
columnTypes.description: The default. Any content — letters, numbers, symbols. No formatting applied. Left-aligned.

columnTypes.name: Number
columnTypes.description: Numeric values. Right-aligned. Sorts numerically. Eligible for auto-sum.

columnTypes.name: Currency
columnTypes.description: Numeric values with a currency symbol prefix. Right-aligned. Sorts numerically. Eligible for auto-sum.

columnTypes.name: Percentage
columnTypes.description: Numeric values with a % suffix. Right-aligned. Sorts numerically.

columnTypes.name: Date
columnTypes.description: Date values. Normalises display format. Sorts chronologically.

steps.heading: Click the type label in the column header
steps.body: Each column header shows the current type — 'Text' by default. Click it to open the column type selector.

steps.heading: Choose a type
steps.body: Select Text, Number, Currency, Percentage, or Date. Every cell in that column updates its formatting immediately.

steps.heading: Add data
steps.body: Type values into the column cells. The formatting applies automatically as you enter content.

useCases: Setting a Price column to Currency so every cell shows the £ or $ symbol consistently
Using Number type for a quantity column so totals sort and sum correctly
Applying Percentage to a completion column for a progress-tracking table
Using Date type so a chronological column sorts correctly rather than alphabetically

_codex_notes.note: columnTypes is an additional array for this page. Render as a 5-card type reference grid between benefits and steps. Each card: icon, name, description, example value shown in a small styled cell preview. The example should be right-aligned for Number/Currency/Percentage, left-aligned for Text, centre-aligned for Date.
